question: [File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getScheduleByIdQuery] Can an unprivileged external user cause a PostgreSQL hash join memory exhaustion by querying GET /api/v1/schedules/{scheduleId} for a schedule with a very large number of signatures, where the getScheduleByIdQuery performs a LEFT JOIN between schedule s, entity e, and transaction_signature ts, and PostgreSQL chooses a hash join strategy that allocates a hash table proportional to the number of signature rows in memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a sustained CPU DoS in Node.js by sending GET /api/v1/schedules requests that return schedules with large key fields, where formatScheduleRow() calls utils.encodeKey(row.key) for every row, and encodeKey performs protobuf deserialization or complex key encoding on each call synchronously, blocking the event loop proportionally to the number of rows and key sizes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a database sequential scan DoS by sending GET /api/v1/schedules with no filters (empty query), causing schedulesMainQuery ('select ... from schedule s') to perform a full table scan with no WHERE clause, followed by entityQuery and signatureQuery also scanning large portions of their respective tables, all within a single request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a database work amplification DoS by sending GET /api/v1/schedules?schedule.id=ne:0, where the ne operator generates a WHERE clause 'where s.schedule_id != $1' that matches nearly all rows in the schedule table, forcing a full table scan and returning the maximum number of rows, then triggering two additional full-scan queries for entities and signatures?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a Node.js string allocation DoS by querying GET /api/v1/schedules at maximum limit, where the positions variable is built by range(1, entityIds.length + 1).map((i) => '$' + i).join(','), creating a string with up to 100 placeholder tokens that is then embedded into both entityQuery and signatureQuery SQL strings, causing repeated string concatenation and allocation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getScheduleById()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a repeated database join DoS by rapidly querying GET /api/v1/schedules/{scheduleId} for the same active schedule ID in a tight loop, where the absence of a cache-control header (getScheduleCacheControlHeader returns {}) for active schedules means no HTTP caching occurs and every request re-executes the three-table LEFT JOIN with json_agg aggregation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a database sort DoS by sending GET /api/v1/schedules?order=desc&schedule.id=lte:9999999999999999, where scheduleOrderQuery generates 'ORDER BY s.schedule_id DESC' and the WHERE clause matches all rows, forcing PostgreSQL to sort the entire schedule table in descending order before applying the LIMIT, consuming CPU and memory proportional to the table size?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: mergeScheduleEntities()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a Node.js GC pressure DoS by sending many concurrent GET /api/v1/schedules requests at maximum limit, where each request creates multiple large intermediate objects in mergeScheduleEntities() (entityMap via reduce, signatureMap via reduce, merged array via map with Object.assign), causing the V8 garbage collector to run frequently and stall the event loop?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a database index scan amplification DoS by sending GET /api/v1/schedules?account.id=gte:1&account.id=lte:999999&schedule.id=gte:1, where extractSqlFromScheduleFilters() generates a WHERE clause with three conditions joined by AND, causing PostgreSQL to evaluate multiple index conditions and potentially perform a merge join or bitmap AND operation that is more expensive than a single-condition query?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a sustained database CPU DoS by sending GET /api/v1/schedules requests that each trigger the signatureQuery with 'json_agg(json_build_object(...) order by ts.consensus_timestamp)' grouped by entity_id, where json_build_object() is called once per signature row and the result is aggregated and sorted, causing O(N log N) database CPU work per request proportional to the total number of signatures across all returned schedules?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getScheduleById()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a database network bandwidth DoS by querying GET /api/v1/schedules/{scheduleId} for a schedule with thousands of signatures, where the getScheduleByIdQuery returns a single row containing a json_agg result with all signatures serialized as a JSON array, causing the database to transmit a very large JSON payload over the internal DB connection, consuming bandwidth between the DB and the REST API process?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a database connection timeout cascade DoS by sending enough concurrent GET /api/v1/schedules requests to exhaust the pool (maxConnections=10), causing subsequent requests to wait for connectionTimeoutMillis (20000ms), and if those waiting requests also time out and retry, creating a thundering herd that permanently saturates the connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: formatScheduleRow()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a Node.js CPU DoS by querying GET /api/v1/schedules for schedules where each row has a large number of signatures, causing formatScheduleRow() to call utils.nsToSecNs(signature.consensus_timestamp) for every signature in the array, where nsToSecNs performs BigInt arithmetic and string formatting on each call, creating O(N) synchronous CPU work per row?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a database lock contention DoS by sending many concurrent GET /api/v1/schedules requests that all read from the same hot schedule rows, causing PostgreSQL to experience high lock contention on shared buffer pages for the schedule, entity, and transaction_signature tables, degrading throughput for all database operations including write operations from the mirror node importer?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a Node.js process memory exhaustion DoS by sending GET /api/v1/schedules requests where the signatureQuery returns large JSON blobs for each schedule, and the pg driver deserializes these JSON strings into JavaScript objects using JSON.parse(), allocating large amounts of heap memory that is not freed until after the entire response is serialized and sent, causing peak memory usage to be several times the response size?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a database planner overhead DoS by sending GET /api/v1/schedules requests with varying combinations of account.id and schedule.id filter operators (eq, gt, gte, lt, lte, ne), where each unique combination generates a structurally different SQL query that cannot reuse a cached query plan, forcing PostgreSQL to re-plan all three queries on every request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getScheduleById()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a sustained DoS by querying GET /api/v1/schedules/{scheduleId} for a schedule that is not yet expired (expirationTime is null and consensusTimestamp is recent), where getScheduleCacheControlHeader() returns {} (no cache), and the request is served fresh from the database on every call, enabling a single attacker to generate unbounded database load with no rate limiting at the caching layer?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a database I/O DoS by sending GET /api/v1/schedules with no filters, where the schedulesMainQuery reads all schedule rows up to the limit, the entityQuery reads all corresponding entity rows, and the signatureQuery reads all corresponding transaction_signature rows, causing three separate sequential I/O operations on potentially large tables and saturating the database disk I/O?
